text: Humans are neither good nor evil; they are simply human.
source: Sayings, collected in Wolof oral tradition; via Wikiquote

text: There is no bad plant and no good plant; it is only the use that is made of it that makes it one or the other.
source: Sayings, collected in Wolof oral tradition; via Wikiquote

text: A man is worth what his word is worth.
source: Sayings, collected in Wolof oral tradition; via Wikiquote

text: The word, once spoken, is like a bird already in flight: you can no longer catch it.
source: Sayings, collected in Wolof oral tradition; via Wikiquote

text: Truth needs no witness; a lie needs a thousand.
source: Sayings, collected in Wolof oral tradition; via Wikiquote

text: Do not speak of your happiness to one who is less fortunate than yourself.
source: Sayings, collected in Wolof oral tradition; via Wikiquote

text: He who does not look ahead will remain behind.
source: Sayings, collected in Wolof oral tradition; via Wikiquote

text: The tree does not refuse its shade to anyone, not even to the man who comes to cut it down.
source: Sayings, collected in Wolof oral tradition; via Wikiquote

text: Knowledge that is not applied is like a field that is not cultivated: it produces nothing.
source: Sayings, collected in Wolof oral tradition; via Wikiquote

text: Better a proud enemy than a false friend.
source: Sayings, collected in Wolof oral tradition; via Wikiquote

text: Do not trust entirely the man who tells you all his secrets; he will tell yours just as readily.
source: Sayings, collected in Wolof oral tradition; via Wikiquote

text: The rain falls on the field of the just and the unjust alike. It is not the falling of rain that makes a man; it is what he does with it.
source: Sayings, collected in Wolof oral tradition; via Wikiquote

text: What you do in the dark will one day speak in the light.
source: Sayings, collected in Wolof oral tradition; via Wikiquote

text: The fool is always certain; it is the wise man who doubts.
source: Sayings, collected in Wolof oral tradition; via Wikiquote

text: Patience is not a gift given to the weak; it is the armor of the strong.
source: Sayings, collected in Wolof oral tradition; via Wikiquote

text: He who knows himself has taken the first step toward knowing everything else.
source: Sayings, collected in Wolof oral tradition; via Wikiquote

text: Do not search for the faults of others before you have finished searching for your own.
source: Sayings, collected in Wolof oral tradition; via Wikiquote

text: The man who acts without thinking is like a man who shoots without aiming: he may hit something, but it will not be what he intended.
source: Sayings, collected in Wolof oral tradition; via Wikiquote

text: One who does not know the depth of the water should not try to cross it. Know your limits before you act; know the ground before you step.
source: Sayings, collected in Wolof oral tradition; via Wikiquote

text: A man without experience walks in daylight as if it were dark.
source: Sayings, collected in Wolof oral tradition; via Wikiquote

text: The lie that causes peace is no better than the truth that causes pain; both lead to the same place in the end.
source: Sayings, collected in Wolof oral tradition; via Wikiquote

text: What you inherit from your father is yours only when you have earned it yourself.
source: Sayings, collected in Wolof oral tradition; via Wikiquote

text: The world is a great field; no man can cultivate it all alone. Work your own portion well.
source: Sayings, collected in Wolof oral tradition; via Wikiquote
